content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To     :     File                                                    Date  :    July 5, 1977


From  :     FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION                     AA-777-915
             Nan Emmerson

Subject:    Combined off-year and post-election statement


On July 5, 1977, I gave the following advice to Shirley Wechsler,
treasurer of Senator David Roberti's campaign committee:

As stated in my June 7, 1977 file memorandum, I advised Mrs. Wechsler
that she need not file separate post-election campaign statements
regarding the 44th and 46th Assembly District special elections since
their deadlines occur after the deadline for filing off-year state-
ments covering through June 30, 1977. I stated that she may file a
combined report with the appropriate filing officers by July 31, 1977
and thereby meet the special elections and off-year filing deadlines.
(An off-year report is required because she filed abbreviated pre-
election statements regarding the special elections.)

Since her off-year statement will include all activity since filing
of the committee's last post-election statement following the 1976
general election, and since the report is serving as post-election
statements for the special elections, I advised, after conferring
with Ted Prim, that following filing of the report, the committee
may begin accumulating its receipts and expenditures anew.

sections.question_synthetic: Can a campaign committee file a combined off-year and post-election statement to meet multiple filing deadlines for special elections and off-year reporting requirements?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC advised that a combined report can be filed by July 31, 1977 to satisfy both the special election post-election deadlines and the off-year statement requirement, and following such filing, the committee may begin accumulating receipts and expenditures anew.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is an internal FPPC memorandum without formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections. The advice and rationale are embedded in the body text. No verbatim Q/C sections available for extraction. High confidence in synthetic summaries based on clear advice narrative.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Can a campaign committee file a combined off-year and post-election statement to meet multiple filing deadlines for special elections and off-year reporting requirements?

CONCLUSION: The FPPC advised that a combined report can be filed by July 31, 1977 to satisfy both the special election post-election deadlines and the off-year statement requirement, and following such filing, the committee may begin accumulating receipts and expenditures anew.

I stated that she may file a combined report with the appropriate filing officers by July 31, 1977 and thereby meet the special elections and off-year filing deadlines. (An off-year report is required because she filed abbreviated pre- election statements regarding the special elections.) Since her off-year statement will include all activity since filing of the committee's last post-election statement following the 1976 general election, and since the report is serving as post-election statements for the special elections, I advised, after conferring with Ted Prim, that following filing of the report, the committee may begin accumulating its receipts and expenditures anew.
embedding.summary: FPPC memorandum dated July 5, 1977 documenting advice given to the treasurer of Senator David Roberti's campaign committee regarding filing a combined off-year and post-election statement to meet multiple deadline requirements.
